summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined a series of recent developments in small UAS activity. A Ukrainian firm has developed the "Chipa" net gun to physically snare FPV drones when other air defenses fail, while another Ukrainian company has won a Pentagon contract in the "Drone Dominance" program. In Iraq, an FPV drone struck the U.S. Victory Base, with an Iran-backed militia claiming responsibility for the first-ever such attack on a U.S. facility in Baghdad. On the front lines, Ukrainian FPV drones have struck Russian logistics targets up to 50 km behind the front in Donetsk and have killed two medics in the Kharkiv region. Russia is rapidly expanding its drone force, aiming for 101,000 drone troops by April, while Ukraine reported hitting 105,200 targets in February.
